---
title: "compare_platforms_tbl"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd)

# `compare_platforms_tbl()`

Compare a Title's Ranking Across Platforms

## Usage

```r
compare_platforms_tbl(
  title,
  platforms = c("netflix", "disney+", "hbo max", "amazon prime", "apple tv+"),
  country_name = "United States",
  date,
  flix_type = "movies",
  token = "FLIX_PATROL"
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `title` | Character. The title to search for in each platform's chart. |
| `platforms` | Character vector. Platform names to compare (default: Netflix, Disney+, HBO Max, Amazon Prime, Apple TV+). |
| `country_name` | Character. Country name. |
| `date` | Date/Character. The chart date. |
| `flix_type` | Character. Content type (default '"movies"'). |
| `token` | Character. API environment variable name. |

## Value

A tibble with columns 'platform', 'rank', 'title', 'date', and chart metadata.
Platforms where the title does not appear return a row with 'rank = NA'.

## Description

Given a title, fetches its Top 10 ranking on multiple platforms for the same
date, returning a single tibble for easy side-by-side comparison.
